title: Amaryllis littoralis
intro:
There is no information on Amaryllis littoralis. It's possible that you may be referring to Amaryllis belladonna or Amaryllis spp. However, I will provide information on Amaryllis belladonna.

Amaryllis belladonna, commonly known as the belladonna lily, is a plant species in the Amaryllidaceae family known for its large, showy flowers. The Amaryllis belladonna has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Amaryllis belladonna include alkaloids such as galantamine, lycorine, and homohaline, which have been shown to have potential therapeutic applications. The most common herbal preparations made with Amaryllis belladonna include extracts, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including Alzheimer's disease, anxiety, and insomnia.
taxonomy: Amaryllis littoralis is classified under the family Amaryllidaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the amaryllis family. This family is further divided into several genera, with Amaryllis being one of them. The Amaryllis genus is native to the Southern Hemisphere, specifically in South Africa and Australia. The classification of Amaryllis littoralis is also closely related to other species within the Amaryllidaceae family, such as the Nerine and Crinum genera. However, the exact classification of Amaryllis littoralis can vary depending on the source and the most recent taxonomic research.
